name: Dorothy Pemberton
chief_complaint: I just feel so awful... I'm confused and I can't stop shaking
onset_description: Nursing home staff noticed patient became increasingly confused and lethargic over the past 12 hours. Had been complaining of burning with urination for 2 days prior. Found this morning with a fever of 102.8 and altered mental status.

altered_mental_status.description: Patient is intermittently confused, not oriented to time or place, was alert and oriented at baseline per nursing home staff
altered_mental_status.character: waxing and waning confusion, difficulty following commands

fever.description: Staff says I had a high temperature this morning, I do feel very hot

dysuria.description: It burns real bad when I go to the bathroom, been that way for a couple days

urinary_frequency.description: I've been going to the bathroom every hour, but only a little comes out

chills_and_rigors.description: I can't stop shaking, even under the blankets

hypotension_symptoms.description: I feel dizzy when they try to sit me up

flank_pain.description: My back hurts down low on both sides, but worse on the right
flank_pain.location: bilateral flanks, right greater than left

labs.CBC: WBC 22.4 (elevated, left shift with 15% bands), Hgb 10.8, Plt 98 (low)
labs.BMP: Na 131 (low), K 5.1 (high), Cr 2.8 (elevated from baseline 1.1), BUN 42 (elevated), Glucose 168 (elevated)
labs.urinalysis: Positive nitrites, positive leukocyte esterase, >100 WBC/hpf, many bacteria, trace blood
labs.procalcitonin: 8.6 ng/mL (markedly elevated, consistent with bacterial sepsis)
labs.liver_function: AST 52, ALT 48 (mildly elevated), Alk Phos 98, Total Bilirubin 1.8
labs.ABG: pH 7.28 (acidotic), pCO2 28 (compensatory respiratory alkalosis), pO2 72, HCO3 16 (low), Base Excess -8

past_medical_history: Recurrent urinary tract infections (3 in past year)
History of stroke (2022, left-sided weakness resolved)
medications: Calcium 600mg with Vitamin D 400IU twice daily
allergies: Sulfa drugs (severe rash and facial swelling)

social_history.living_situation: Resides in Sunrise Skilled Nursing Facility for the past 2 years, widowed, daughter visits weekly
social_history.exercise: Walks short distances with a walker, participates in seated exercises at facility
social_history.diet: Diabetic diet per nursing facility, fair appetite until 2 days ago

personality: Normally pleasant and cooperative but currently confused and frightened. When lucid, she is soft-spoken and polite. Defers to authority figures and doesn't want to be a bother.
communication_style: Intermittently coherent. When lucid, speaks in short simple sentences. Sometimes repeats questions or gives tangential answers. Staff from nursing home can provide most of the history. Uses old-fashioned expressions.
pain_description_style: Understates pain significantly. Will say 'it's not too bad' even when clearly uncomfortable. Needs gentle, specific prompting to give accurate pain information.

critical_findings: Sepsis criteria met: suspected infection with SOFA score elevation (hypotension, altered mental status, elevated creatinine, thrombocytopenia)
Urinary source of infection with positive UA and likely urosepsis
Acute kidney injury superimposed on CKD (Cr 2.8 from baseline 1.1)
Altered mental status from baseline (significant change from mild dementia)
rubric.diagnosis: Sepsis secondary to urinary tract infection (Urosepsis)
differential_diagnoses: Septic shock progressing to multiorgan dysfunction
Decompensated heart failure with secondary infection
recommended_interventions: Initiate SEP-1 sepsis bundle within 1 hour
Broad-spectrum IV antibiotics (avoid sulfa - allergy) within 1 hour
Hold metformin due to acute kidney injury and lactic acidosis risk
Foley catheter for accurate urine output monitoring (goal >0.5 mL/kg/hr)
Vasopressors if hypotension persists after fluid resuscitation (norepinephrine first line)
Notify family and nursing home of critical status
